samples.gvd:
module synchronizer(
    input clk_a,
    input clk_b,
    input arstn,
    input brstn,
    input data_in,
    input data_en,
    output reg dataout
);

reg [1:0] sync_a;
reg [2:0] sync_b;

always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
    if (!arstn) begin
        sync_a <= 2'b00;
    end else begin
        sync_a <= {sync_a[0], data_in};
    end
end

always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
    if (!brstn) begin
        sync_b <= 3'b000;
    end else begin
        sync_b <= {sync_b[1:0], sync_a[1]};
    end
end

always @(posedge clk_b) begin
    if (sync_b[2] && sync_b[1] && !sync_b[0]) begin
        dataout <= sync_a[1];
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer. The data_en input is synchronous to clk_a and the data_in input is constant during the period when data_en=1. So, sample the data_in when data_en=1 and synchronize it to clk_b. The data change frequency of data_in is very low. The change interval between two adjacent data is at least 10 clk_b clock cycles. Note that the data_en is sampled at clk_b and the output is also at clk_b.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N" value="4">Size of data_in and dataout.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="data_reg" width="[N-1:0]">Data input register.</reg>
        <reg id="en_data_reg">Enable data register.</reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_one">Enable control register.</reg>
        <reg id="en_clap_two">Enable control register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <parablock id="data_reg">
            <always_comb>
                if (~arstn) begin
                    <non_blocking>data_reg <= 0;</non_blocking>
                end
                else begin
                    <non_blocking>data_reg <= data_in;</non_blocking>
                end
            </always_comb>
        </parablock>
        <parablock id="en_data_reg">
            <always_comb>
                if (~arstn) begin
                    <non_blocking>en_data_reg <= 0;</non_blocking>
                end
                else begin
                    <non_blocking>en_data_reg <= data_en;</non_blocking>
                end
            </always_comb>
        </parablock>
        <parablock id="en_clap_one">
            <always_comb>
                if (~brstn) begin
                    <non_blocking>en_clap_one <= 0;</non_blocking>
                end
                else begin
                    <non_blocking>en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;</non_blocking>
                end
            </always_comb>
        </parablock>
        <parablock id="en_clap_two">
            <always_comb>
                if (~brstn) begin
                    <non_blocking>en_clap_two <= 0;</non_blocking>
                end
                else begin
                    <non_blocking>en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;</non_blocking>
                end
            </always_comb>
        </parablock>
        <parablock id="dataout">
            <always_comb>
                if (~brstn) begin
                    <non_blocking>dataout <= 0;</non_blocking>
                end
                else begin
                    if (en_clap_two) begin
                        <non_blocking>dataout <= data_reg;</non_blocking>
                    end
                end
            </always_comb>
        </parablock>
    </implementation>
</module>
